name: Active and passive hyperthermia did not significantly increase PBMC HSP70
summary: A small human physiology study is a caution against assuming heat exposure reliably induces intracellular HSP70 in circulating immune cells.
statement: In six healthy young men, neither active nor passive hyperthermia significantly increased intracellular HSP70 in PBMCs at 0 or 4 hours after exposure.
time_horizon: Immediate and 4-hour post-exposure PBMC HSP70 measurements
quantitative_note: Peak HSP70 fold-change from rest was not significantly increased after active hyperthermia or passive hyperthermia.
caveats: The study was very small and short-term.
PBMC intracellular HSP70 may not represent tissue proteostasis or repeated heat-exposure adaptation.
The result does not rule out other heat-shock biomarkers or protocols.
